The classification of Leersia perrieri reflects its evolutionary relationships with other grasses.

intro: Leersia perrieri, also known as the Madagascar rice grass, is a species of grass native to Madagascar. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering various health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Leersia perrieri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Madagascar medicine, Leersia perrieri is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for skin and wound care.
